dimensions.name: Schedule Slippage
reasons: More than 10% of tasks slipped past baseline (64.0%)
Phase-level milestone is delayed by more than 2 weeks
Worst task variance is 81 days behind baseline (worst offender: Phase 2 - P2P)

dimensions.name: Milestone Health
reasons: On/ahead of pace: project is 71.0% complete vs. 58.9% elapsed time
6 'Not Started' task(s) whose planned start date has passed

dimensions.name: Critical Path Risk
reasons: 11 critical path tasks are at risk — represents a high risk to key dates
Examples: Setup PRODUCTION environment (Integration) - Zycus, Schedule for quality check, Perform quality checks, PSRs as per quality checklist and submit to QC team

dimensions.name: Blockers
reasons: 136 open blocker tasks detected (exceeds red threshold of 4)
3 client-owned blocker(s) stalled for more than 2 weeks
Blockers: Supplier Notification template (On Hold, At Risk = High) | OTK to share D&B creds (On Hold) | Enable D & B App (On Hold)

dimensions.name: Stakeholder Sentiment
reasons: 4 client-owned tasks are past their end date
Representative concern: Due to parallel phase 1 activities onsite workshop dates are impacted

dimensions.name: Budget Burn
reasons: Budget/cost data is not present in the current project plans. Excluded from scoring.

rag.perception_gap: PM-reported schedule health is 'Green' while signal-based analysis computes 'Red' — flagged for reconciliation, not overridden
top_reasons: Hard Override to Red: a phase-level milestone end date has passed with the phase still 'In Progress'
More than 10% of tasks slipped past baseline (64.0%)
Phase-level milestone is delayed by more than 2 weeks
Worst task variance is 81 days behind baseline (worst offender: Phase 2 - P2P)
11 critical path tasks are at risk — represents a high risk to key dates
caveats: PM-reported % complete (71%) diverges from the task-level computed % complete (54%) by 17% — worth reconciling with the PM

narrative: The project is in a Red status because more than 10 % of tasks have slipped past baseline, with 64 % of tasks behind schedule and the worst task 81 days late in Phase 2 P2P. Critical path risk is high, with 11 tasks at risk—including key setup and quality‑check activities—and 136 open blocker tasks, three of which are client‑owned and stalled for over two weeks, far exceeding the red threshold. While milestone health shows the project is 71 % complete versus 58.9 % elapsed time, the overall health is red due to the schedule slippage, critical‑path risk, and stakeholder sentiment issues, with four client‑owned tasks past their end date. Data quality caveats note that the PM’s reported 71 % completion differs by 17 % from the task‑level 54 % and that the PM’s green schedule health conflicts with the signal‑based red, so reconciliation is needed; budget burn is grey because cost data is missing.
